topic: Two friends plan separate birthday parties on the same day
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in Maya’s inbox like a physical blow. *“Re: Birthday Plans – Separate Events.”* Her stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath her ribs. Liam, her best friend since kindergarten, had organized the whole thing—a joint celebration, a grand gesture they’d both envisioned for years. Now, he was splitting the day. She reread the message three times, the words blurring into a mocking script. When she finally typed a reply, her fingers trembled slightly over the keyboard. “Okay,” she managed, the sound thin and reedy. She couldn't bring herself to voice the sheer awkwardness churning inside her. She just wanted to disappear.

[story 2]

"So, you're ditching the main event?" Chloe asked, her tone light, almost breezy. Mark watched her, a slow, unpleasant tightening spreading across his jawline. He felt a strange, hot flush creep up his neck. The reality of the situation—that his carefully curated surprise party was now a solitary affair—hit him with unexpected force. He fiddled with the cuff of his shirt, his gaze fixed on a speck of dust on the floor. "Yeah, well, I think I need some space," he mumbled, the words tasting like ash. He could feel the eyes of the other guests on him, cataloging the shift in his demeanor, the sudden withdrawal. It felt like being put on a pedestal, only to have the pedestal suddenly snatched away.

[story 3]

Sarah stared at the calendar, the circled date mocking her with its bright, cheerful color. Liam had been so enthusiastic, so certain about the unified celebration. Now, the confirmation text arrived: two separate invitations, two different venues, two entirely different atmospheres. A wave of profound deflation washed over her, leaving her feeling strangely lightheaded. She picked up her phone, the screen reflecting a face that seemed too cheerful, too carefree. She took a deep, shaky breath, trying to smooth the frantic energy vibrating through her limbs. "Right," she said, forcing a smile that felt brittle on her face. "I guess I'll just focus on my own plans."
